extra.reasoning: The property requires that rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are never jointly true. rec_drug_b is set true only by rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50): IF diabetes. rec_drug_a is set true only by rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50): IF (diabetes AND low_ejection_fraction AND prior_gi_bleed AND hypertension AND renal_impairment AND g6pd_deficiency). The conjunction itself includes diabetes as a conjunct, so whenever rec_drug_a fires, diabetes is true, which necessarily also fires rec_b_from_trigger. No other rules affect these two outputs (the rec_c rules are irrelevant), so there is no competing rule to override either via priority, and defaults only apply to unset outputs. Setting all six conjuncts true (diabetes, low_ejection_fraction, prior_gi_bleed, hypertension, renal_impairment, g6pd_deficiency) makes both rec_drug_a := true and rec_drug_b := true simultaneously, violating mutual exclusion. The other three inputs (qt_prolongation, heart_failure, hepatic_impairment) only influence rec_drug_c and are set false.
